final_response:
Accra, the capital city of Ghana, is a major metropolis in Sub-Saharan Africa. It covers a total area of 20.4 square kilometers, with an urban area of 199,400,000.0 square meters and a metropolitan area spanning 3,245,000,000.0 square meters. The city sits at an elevation of 60.96 meters above sea level. Accra has a total population of 284,124, while its metropolitan area is home to 5,455,692 people. Residents of the city are known as Accrans.

Accra uses the area code 030 and its postal codes include GA, GL, and GZ. The city operates on Greenwich Mean Time (GMT) with a UTC offset of +0. As both a capital city and a metropolis, Accra is a central hub in Ghana.

The Ga and Dangme languages are spoken in Accra, reflecting the city's linguistic diversity. Accra is not only the capital and largest city of Ghana, but it also served as the capital of the former Gold Coast (British colony) and is central to the history of Ghana.

Several educational institutions are located in Accra, including the Sikkim Manipal University, Ghana Learning Centre, and Wisconsin International University College. The city is also home to the Presbyterian Senior High School, Osu, Sarbah Hall, and Uniiq FM. Important organizations such as the Ministry of Women and Children's Affairs, Bishop Bowers School, Braves of Customs, and the National Liberation Council are based in Accra. The Environmental Protection Agency (Ghana), Ghana Airports Company Limited, and Ghana News Agency all have their city locations in Accra. The headquarters of the Progress Party (Ghana), Meridian Airways, and Metro TV (Ghana) are also situated in the city.

Accra is served by companies such as GIG Mobility, Surfline Communications, and BusyInternet. The city is the nearest urban center to Achimota Forest and Shai Hills Resource Reserve. It also hosts the garrisons of the President's Own Guard Regiment, the Ghana Regiment, and the West Africa Command. Accra is the home ground for the 2020–21 Accra Great Olympics F.C. season, Vision F.C., and Liberty Professionals F.C.

Juliet Ibrahim has received an award in Accra. The city is the hometown of notable individuals such as Samini, Barima Sidney, and Becca, the Ghanaian singer. Benjamin Asante, Derek Abrefa, and Kwesi Amissah-Arthur have their residences in Accra. It is also the birthplace of Prince Amponsah, Prince Baffoe, and Prince Gyasi. Carl Henry Clerk, Prince Yawson, and Roger Joseph Felli all passed away in Accra.

Accra, the capital city of Ghana, is a major metropolis in Sub-Saharan Africa. It covers a total area of 20.4 square kilometers, with an urban area of 199,400,000.0 square meters and a metropolitan area spanning 3,245,000,000.0 square meters. The city sits at an elevation of 60.96 meters above sea level. Accra has a total population of 284,124, while its metropolitan area is home to 5,455,692 people. Residents of the city are known as Accrans.

Accra uses the area code 030 and its postal codes include GA, GL, and GZ. The city operates on Greenwich Mean Time (GMT) with a UTC offset of +0. As both a capital city and a metropolis, Accra is a central hub in Ghana.

The Ga and Dangme languages are spoken in Accra, reflecting the city's linguistic diversity. Accra is not only the capital and largest city of Ghana, but it also served as the capital of the former Gold Coast (British colony) and is central to the history of Ghana.

Several educational institutions are located in Accra, including the Sikkim Manipal University, Ghana Learning Centre, and Wisconsin International University College. The city is also home to the Presbyterian Senior High School, Osu, Sarbah Hall, and Uniiq FM. Important organizations such as the Ministry of Women and Children's Affairs, Bishop Bowers School, Braves of Customs, and the National Liberation Council are based in Accra. The Environmental Protection Agency (Ghana), Ghana Airports Company Limited, and Ghana News Agency all have their city locations in Accra. The headquarters of the Progress Party (Ghana), Meridian Airways, and Metro TV (Ghana) are also situated in the city.

Accra is served by companies such as GIG Mobility, Surfline Communications, and BusyInternet. The city is the nearest urban center to Achimota Forest and Shai Hills Resource Reserve. It also hosts the garrisons of the President's Own Guard Regiment, the Ghana Regiment, and the West Africa Command. Accra is the home ground for the 2020–21 Accra Great Olympics F.C. season, Vision F.C., and Liberty Professionals F.C.

Juliet Ibrahim has received an award in Accra. The city is the hometown of notable individuals such as Samini, Barima Sidney, and Becca, the Ghanaian singer. Benjamin Asante, Derek Abrefa, and Kwesi Amissah-Arthur have their residences in Accra. It is also the birthplace of Prince Amponsah, Prince Baffoe, and Prince Gyasi. Carl Henry Clerk, Prince Yawson, and Roger Joseph Felli all passed away in Accra.
